first_line_drugs.notes: Symptomatic (Na <125 with seizures/encephalopathy): 4–6 ml/kg IV over 30 min; may repeat 2 ml/kg over 15 min if seizures persist; target rise 5–10 mEq/L
Asymptomatic (Na <125): correct slowly using calculated Na deficit over 4–24 hrs
50–75% of maintenance fluids in SIADH-related hyponatremia

second_line_drugs.notes: Used in hypovolemic hyponatremia to restore volume
1 mg/kg IV in selected cases (SIADH with fluid overload)

counselling: Low sodium levels can affect brain function — सोडियम कम होने से दिमाग की कार्यप्रणाली प्रभावित हो सकती है।
Treatment will be done slowly to avoid complications — इलाज धीरे-धीरे किया जाएगा ताकि कोई दिक्कत न हो।
Fluid intake may be restricted as advised — डॉक्टर की सलाह अनुसार पानी/तरल पदार्थ सीमित रखें।
Regular blood tests are required for monitoring — निगरानी के लिए नियमित ब्लड टेस्ट ज़रूरी हैं।
Follow-up is important to identify underlying cause — असली कारण पता लगाने के लिए फॉलो-अप ज़रूरी है।
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist if symptomatic hyponatremia or rapid sodium fall
hospitalisation_criteria: Na <125 mEq/L, neurological symptoms, seizures, or need for IV correction
notes: Avoid rapid correction. Increase Na should not exceed 10–12 mEq/L/day or 0.5 mEq/L/hr to prevent osmotic demyelination.
source: IAP Guidelines 2024, Pediatric Electrolyte Management Protocols
guideline_changes: Emphasis on controlled correction rate and early use of 3% saline in symptomatic cases
expected_course: Improvement within 24–48 hrs with proper correction
key_clinical_points: Always identify and treat underlying cause (e.g., SIADH)

severity_assessment.severe: Na <125 mEq/L with seizures, coma, encephalopathy
